title: Former Richard Stallman Colleague Now Argues for Open AI Models Too - Slashdot
excerpt: Long-time Slashdot reader theodp writes: Recalling his initial resistance to free and open software, billionaire computer scientist David Siegel argues vigorously in FORTUNE that the stakes are too high to let AI become increasingly closed. "In the 1980s, I had the chance to spend several years ar...
content_html:
Long-time Slashdot reader theodp writes: Recalling his initial resistance to free and open software, billionaire computer scientist David Siegel argues vigorously in FORTUNE that the stakes are too high to let AI become increasingly closed. "In the 1980s, I had the chance to spend several years arguing about free and open software, what we now call open source, with the founder of the movement, Richard Stallman. My office at the MIT AI Lab was next door to his. Stallman's position was that the source code to software should be free for everyone to use, learn from, and improve. Software encapsulates knowledge, he argued, and no one should lock something so fundamental away. To hide software inside a company was to hide knowledge itself... What I missed was that software was not just a commercial asset; it was a body of knowledge, and bodies of knowledge grow stronger when they are shared. After about two years of on-and-off debate, Stallman convinced me I was wrong."

"Now the AI fight is the same — only bigger," advises Siegel. "AI is software, and AI is increasingly closed. The frontier models — the most advanced, cutting-edge AI systems — are closed completely and the trend is accelerating. Viable open alternatives are few and far between." So, what to do...? "Yes, frontier models keep getting bigger and more expensive — that arms race may well stay with the giants. But open source AI does not have to match their scale to be useful. Much of what the world needs probably does not require the absolute frontier. And where keeping a credible open option does demand serious compute, that is precisely the kind of public good worth paying for.

"What's missing is not a path but will. The government, the private sector, and nonprofits should invest heavily in free and open source AI — the way they once invested in open software: public compute grants for open research, corporate and philanthropic support for universities and nonprofits doing the work, and a simple rule that AI built with public money is open by default.

"We have run this experiment before. We know how it turns out. Let's not unlearn it."

"In the 1980s, I had the chance to spend several years arguing about free and open software, what we now call open source, with the founder of the movement, Richard Stallman. My office at the MIT AI Lab was next door to his. Stallman's position was that the source code to software should be free for everyone to use, learn from, and improve. Software encapsulates knowledge, he argued, and no one should lock something so fundamental away. To hide software inside a company was to hide knowledge itself... What I missed was that software was not just a commercial asset; it was a body of knowledge, and bodies of knowledge grow stronger when they are shared. After about two years of on-and-off debate, Stallman convinced me I was wrong." "Now the AI fight is the same — only bigger," advises Siegel. "AI is software, and AI is increasingly closed. The frontier models — the most advanced, cutting-edge AI systems — are closed completely and the trend is accelerating. Viable open alternatives are few and far between." So, what to do...? "Yes, frontier models keep getting bigger and more expensive — that arms race may well stay with the giants. But open source AI does not have to match their scale to be useful. Much of what the world needs probably does not require the absolute frontier. And where keeping a credible open option does demand serious compute, that is precisely the kind of public good worth paying for. "What's missing is not a path but will. The government, the private sector, and nonprofits should invest heavily in free and open source AI — the way they once invested in open software: public compute grants for open research, corporate and philanthropic support for universities and nonprofits doing the work, and a simple rule that AI built with public money is open by default. "We have run this experiment before. We know how it turns out. Let's not unlearn it."

sentences.en: Long-time Slashdot reader theodp writes: Recalling his initial resistance to free and open software, billionaire computer scientist David Siegel argues vigorously in FORTUNE that the stakes are too high to let AI become increasingly closed.
sentences.zh: Slashdot 的长期读者 theodp 写道： 亿万富翁计算机科学家大卫·西格尔 (David Siegel) 回想起他最初对自由和开放软件的抵制，他在《财富》杂志上强烈主张，让人工智能变得越来越封闭的风险太高了。

sentences.en: "In the 1980s, I had the chance to spend several years arguing about free and open software, what we now call open source, with the founder of the movement, Richard Stallman.
sentences.zh: “在 20 世纪 80 年代，我有机会花了几年时间与该运动的创始人 Richard Stallman 讨论自由和开放软件，即我们现在所说的开源。

sentences.en: My office at the MIT AI Lab was next door to his.
sentences.zh: 我在麻省理工学院人工智能实验室的办公室就在他的隔壁。

sentences.en: Stallman's position was that the source code to software should be free for everyone to use, learn from, and improve.
sentences.zh: 斯托曼的立场是软件的源代码应该免费供每个人使用、学习和改进。

sentences.en: Software encapsulates knowledge, he argued, and no one should lock something so fundamental away.
sentences.zh: 他认为，软件封装了知识，任何人都不应该将如此基本的东西锁起来。

sentences.en: To hide software inside a company was to hide knowledge itself...
sentences.zh: 在公司内部隐藏软件就是隐藏知识本身......

sentences.en: What I missed was that software was not just a commercial asset; it was a body of knowledge, and bodies of knowledge grow stronger when they are shared.
sentences.zh: 我错过的是软件不仅仅是一种商业资产；它也是一种商业资产。它是一个知识体系，而知识体系在分享时会变得更加强大。

sentences.en: After about two years of on-and-off debate, Stallman convinced me I was wrong."
sentences.zh: 经过大约两年的断断续续的辩论，斯托曼说服我，我错了。”

sentences.en: "Now the AI fight is the same — only bigger," advises Siegel.
sentences.zh: “现在人工智能的战斗是一样的——只是规模更大，”西格尔建议道。

sentences.en: "AI is software, and AI is increasingly closed.
sentences.zh: “人工智能是软件，而且人工智能越来越封闭。

sentences.en: The frontier models — the most advanced, cutting-edge AI systems — are closed completely and the trend is accelerating.
sentences.zh: 前沿模型——最先进、最前沿的人工智能系统——已经完全封闭，而且这一趋势正在加速。

sentences.en: Viable open alternatives are few and far between."
sentences.zh: 可行的开放替代方案少之又少。”

sentences.en: So, what to do...?
sentences.zh: 那么，该怎么办...？

sentences.en: "Yes, frontier models keep getting bigger and more expensive — that arms race may well stay with the giants.
sentences.zh: “是的，前沿模型变得越来越大、越来越昂贵——军备竞赛很可能会持续到巨头们身上。

sentences.en: But open source AI does not have to match their scale to be useful.
sentences.zh: 但开源人工智能并不一定要达到其规模才能发挥作用。

sentences.en: Much of what the world needs probably does not require the absolute frontier.
sentences.zh: 世界所需的许多东西可能并不需要绝对边界。

sentences.en: And where keeping a credible open option does demand serious compute, that is precisely the kind of public good worth paying for.
sentences.zh: 保持可信的开放选项确实需要认真的计算，这正是值得付出代价的公共利益。

sentences.en: "What's missing is not a path but will.
sentences.zh: “缺少的不是道路，而是意志。

sentences.en: The government, the private sector, and nonprofits should invest heavily in free and open source AI — the way they once invested in open software: public compute grants for open research, corporate and philanthropic support for universities and nonprofits doing the work, and a simple rule that AI built with public money is open by default.
sentences.zh: 政府、私营部门和非营利组织应该大力投资免费和开源的人工智能——就像他们曾经投资开放软件的方式一样：为开放研究提供公共计算拨款，为大学和非营利组织提供相关工作的企业和慈善支持，以及用公共资金构建的人工智能默认开放的简单规则。

sentences.en: "We have run this experiment before.
sentences.zh: “我们以前做过这个实验。

sentences.en: We know how it turns out.
sentences.zh: 我们知道结果如何。

sentences.en: Let's not unlearn it."
sentences.zh: 我们不要忘记它。”

Much of what the world needs probably does not require the absolute frontier. And where keeping a credible open option does demand serious compute, that is precisely the kind of public good worth paying for.

sentences.zh: 前沿模型——最先进、最前沿的人工智能系统——已经完全封闭，而且这一趋势正在加速。

sentences.en: Viable open alternatives are few and far between."
sentences.zh: 可行的开放替代方案少之又少。”

sentences.zh: 那么，该怎么办...？

sentences.zh: “是的，前沿模型变得越来越大、越来越昂贵——军备竞赛很可能会持续到巨头们身上。

sentences.en: But open source AI does not have to match their scale to be useful.
sentences.zh: 但开源人工智能并不一定要达到其规模才能发挥作用。

sentences.en: Much of what the world needs probably does not require the absolute frontier.
sentences.zh: 世界所需的许多东西可能并不需要绝对边界。

sentences.en: And where keeping a credible open option does demand serious compute, that is precisely the kind of public good worth paying for.
sentences.zh: 保持可信的开放选项确实需要认真的计算，这正是值得付出代价的公共利益。

blocks.html_en: "What's missing is not a path but will. The government, the private sector, and nonprofits should invest heavily in free and open source AI — the way they once invested in open software: public compute grants for open research, corporate and philanthropic support for universities and nonprofits doing the work, and a simple rule that AI built with public money is open by default.

sentences.en: "What's missing is not a path but will.
sentences.zh: “缺少的不是道路，而是意志。

sentences.en: The government, the private sector, and nonprofits should invest heavily in free and open source AI — the way they once invested in open software: public compute grants for open research, corporate and philanthropic support for universities and nonprofits doing the work, and a simple rule that AI built with public money is open by default.
sentences.zh: 政府、私营部门和非营利组织应该大力投资免费和开源的人工智能——就像他们曾经投资开放软件的方式一样：为开放研究提供公共计算拨款，为大学和非营利组织提供相关工作的企业和慈善支持，以及用公共资金构建的人工智能默认开放的简单规则。

sentences.en: "We have run this experiment before.
sentences.zh: “我们以前做过这个实验。

sentences.en: We know how it turns out.
sentences.zh: 我们知道结果如何。

sentences.en: Let's not unlearn it."
sentences.zh: 我们不要忘记它。”

content_html_zh: Slashdot 的长期读者 theodp 写道： 亿万富翁计算机科学家大卫·西格尔 (David Siegel) 回想起他最初对自由和开放软件的抵制，他在《财富》杂志上强烈主张，让人工智能变得越来越封闭的风险太高了。 “在 20 世纪 80 年代，我有机会花了几年时间与该运动的创始人 Richard Stallman 讨论自由和开放软件，即我们现在所说的开源。 我在麻省理工学院人工智能实验室的办公室就在他的隔壁。 斯托曼的立场是软件的源代码应该免费供每个人使用、学习和改进。 他认为，软件封装了知识，任何人都不应该将如此基本的东西锁起来。 在公司内部隐藏软件就是隐藏知识本身...... 我错过的是软件不仅仅是一种商业资产；它也是一种商业资产。它是一个知识体系，而知识体系在分享时会变得更加强大。 经过大约两年的断断续续的辩论，斯托曼说服我，我错了。” “现在人工智能的战斗是一样的——只是规模更大，”西格尔建议道。 “人工智能是软件，而且人工智能越来越封闭。 前沿模型——最先进、最前沿的人工智能系统——已经完全封闭，而且这一趋势正在加速。 可行的开放替代方案少之又少。” 那么，该怎么办...？ “是的，前沿模型变得越来越大、越来越昂贵——军备竞赛很可能会持续到巨头们身上。 但开源人工智能并不一定要达到其规模才能发挥作用。 世界所需的许多东西可能并不需要绝对边界。 保持可信的开放选项确实需要认真的计算，这正是值得付出代价的公共利益。 “缺少的不是道路，而是意志。 政府、私营部门和非营利组织应该大力投资免费和开源的人工智能——就像他们曾经投资开放软件的方式一样：为开放研究提供公共计算拨款，为大学和非营利组织提供相关工作的企业和慈善支持，以及用公共资金构建的人工智能默认开放的简单规则。 “我们以前做过这个实验。 我们知道结果如何。 我们不要忘记它。”
